title: Quiz: Beyond VQE
description: Test your understanding of advanced quantum chemistry algorithms

questions.question: What is the main advantage of Quantum Phase Estimation (QPE) over VQE?

options.text: It provides exponentially precise eigenvalues without optimization
It works better on NISQ devices

questions.explanation: QPE provides exponentially precise eigenvalues deterministically without needing iterative optimization. However, it requires much deeper circuits that are only feasible on fault-tolerant quantum computers.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Quantum Subspace Expansion (QSE)?

options.text: To reduce the number of qubits needed
To improve ground state energy and obtain excited states from a VQE result

questions.explanation: QSE builds a subspace of states by applying excitation operators to the VQE ground state, then solves a generalized eigenvalue problem to improve the ground state estimate and obtain excited states.

questions.question: Why can't imaginary time evolution e^{-Hτ} be directly implemented on a quantum computer?

options.text: The operator is not unitary (doesn't preserve norm)
It takes too long to compute
It only works for classical systems

questions.explanation: The imaginary time evolution operator e^{-Hτ} is not unitary because it doesn't preserve the norm of the state. Quantum computers can only directly implement unitary operations, so QITE approximates this with unitary operations.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of QITE (Quantum Imaginary Time Evolution)?

options.text: It uses fewer measurements than VQE
It works only for excited states

questions.explanation: QITE systematically projects the state toward the ground state without requiring variational optimization. The evolution is determined by solving linear systems at each step, not by optimizing a cost function.

questions.question: Quantum Phase Estimation is suitable for current NISQ devices because it uses shallow circuits.
questions.explanation: False. QPE requires very deep circuits (exponentially deep in the precision desired) and is not suitable for NISQ devices. It requires fault-tolerant quantum computers to achieve its precision advantages.

questions.question: What is quantum embedding in the context of hybrid methods?

options.text: Storing quantum states in classical memory
Treating part of a system with quantum methods while the rest is classical
Embedding classical algorithms in quantum circuits

questions.explanation: Quantum embedding divides a large system into an 'active region' treated with quantum methods (like VQE) and an 'environment' treated classically (DFT, MM). This allows quantum treatment of challenging regions while managing resource costs.

questions.question: In variational quantum dynamics, what determines how parameters θ(t) evolve?
questions.explanation: The McLachlan variational principle minimizes the difference between the variational ansatz evolution and the exact Schrödinger evolution. This leads to equations M θ̇ = V that determine how parameters evolve in time.

questions.question: Which are valid methods for obtaining excited states from quantum computers? (Select all)
questions.explanation: QSE, qEOM, and QPE can all provide excited state energies. QSE diagonalizes in an expanded subspace, qEOM uses excitation operators, and QPE can measure any eigenvalue if the eigenstate has overlap with the initial state. ZNE is an error mitigation technique, not an excited state method.
